item.title: France Expands Nuclear Deterrence Pact to Scandinavia as Norway Opens Talks
item.description: Norway has become the latest ally to discuss joining France's extended nuclear deterrence initiative, as European defense cooperation deepens amid growing security concerns.

item.title: India Issues Fresh Travel Advisory for Iran, Urges Nationals to Leave Immediately
item.description: India has reiterated its travel advisory for Iran amid escalating regional tensions, advising Indian nationals to avoid travel and urging those present to depart immediately.

item.title: OPEC+ Approves Fourth Oil Output Hike Since Hormuz Closure
item.description: OPEC+ approved another production increase despite the Strait of Hormuz disruption, with analysts noting much of the added supply may remain undeliverable to global markets.

item.title: EU Defence Ministers Convene in Nicosia With Ukraine War Set to Dominate Agenda
item.description: The EU's 27 defence ministers are gathering in Cyprus on Monday, with the ongoing Ukraine conflict expected to be the primary focus of discussions.

item.title: Gold Prices Decline as Middle East Reescalation Dampens Safe-Haven Demand
item.description: Gold prices began the week with losses despite renewed Middle East tensions, as market dynamics shift amid the ongoing Israel-Iran conflict.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strike kills one in Russian-annexed Crimea
item.description: A Ukrainian drone strike killed one person in Russian-annexed Crimea, according to Moscow-installed officials, as attacks continue on the peninsula.

item.title: Xi Jinping hails new era in China-North Korea ties ahead of summit with Kim
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for his first visit in seven years, seeking to reassert China's influence amid shifting regional dynamics.

item.title: Chinese warships shadow global executives at Taiwan AI tech gathering
item.description: Chinese military vessels monitored a major technology conference in Taiwan attended by global executives, underscoring ongoing tensions in the Taiwan Strait.

item.title: Iran says Hormuz Strait will reopen with transit fees
item.description: Iran's envoy to Moscow stated the Strait of Hormuz would reopen with transit fees imposed, signaling potential conditions for resuming critical oil shipping routes.

item.title: South Korean President Lee rules out nuclear armament, affirms US alliance
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung stated South Korea would not pursue nuclear weapons, emphasizing the continued importance of the US security alliance amid regional tensions.

item.title: Cambodia's scam center crackdown fails to dismantle industry
item.description: Cambodia's campaign against scam centers has failed to meaningfully impact the criminal industry, according to Amnesty International, with victims still being exploited.

item.title: Dollar climbs to two-month peak as Fed rate hike bets ramp up
item.description: The US dollar reached a two-month high as market expectations for Federal Reserve rate increases intensified amid persistent inflation concerns.

item.title: China exports set for strong May on front-loaded orders and chip demand
item.description: Chinese exports are projected to show robust growth in May, driven by front-loaded orders and sustained semiconductor demand despite global trade tensions.

item.title: Israel Hits Iranian Targets Despite Trump Warning to Netanyahu
item.description: Israel conducted strikes on Iranian targets including a petrochemical facility, despite US President Trump's calls for restraint to preserve ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: Tsunami Alerts Across Asia After Philippines Quake, Evacuations Underway
item.description: A powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck off the southern Philippines, triggering tsunami warnings across multiple countries and prompting coastal evacuations.

item.title: Zelensky and European Leaders Set Five Conditions for Just Peace
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelensky met with UK, German, and French leaders in London to establish conditions for potential peace negotiations with Russia.

item.title: IRGC Claims Israel Used Air-Launched Ballistic Missiles in Iran Strike
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed Israel deployed air-launched ballistic missiles in recent strikes on Iranian territory, marking an escalation in capabilities.

item.title: Indonesian Political Heavyweights Position as Prabowo Falters
item.description: Indonesian political figures are repositioning amid signs of weakness in President Prabowo's administration, with opposition figures sensing an opening.

item.title: Philippines Verifying at Least Five Deaths After 7.8 Magnitude Quake
item.description: Philippine authorities are confirming at least five fatalities following the powerful earthquake that struck Mindanao, with assessments ongoing.

item.title: Pashinyan Declares Victory in Armenia Election, Cementing Western Pivot
item.description: Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's party claimed victory in parliamentary elections with 57% of early results, consolidating his pro-Western orientation.

item.title: OPEC+ Approves Fourth Oil Output Hike Since Hormuz Closure
item.description: OPEC+ approved another production increase, marking the fourth quota hike since the Strait of Hormuz closure disrupted global oil supplies.

item.title: European Leaders Support Ukraine Ceasefire Talks, Bolster Military Aid
item.description: Leaders from the UK, Germany, and France expressed support for potential Ukraine-Russia ceasefire negotiations while pledging continued military support.

item.title: South Korea Halts Trading as KOSPI Plunges Over 8% in Tech-Led Selloff
item.description: South Korea's stock exchange halted trading after the KOSPI index dropped more than 8%, driven by a massive technology sector selloff.

item.title: Japan Ground Self-Defense Force Holds Live-Fire Exercises With 3,000 Troops
item.description: Japan's Ground Self-Defense Force conducted large-scale live-fire exercises involving approximately 3,000 troops, showcasing military readiness amid regional tensions.

item.title: Philippines Hit by 7.8 Magnitude Earthquake, Tsunami Warnings Issued
item.description: A powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck off the southern Philippines, prompting tsunami warnings across the Pacific region.
